meta.title: Vesta — Family life across countries, languages, and generations
meta.description: The AI-native family app for households that live across countries, languages, and generations. Built for expat families first. Join the waitlist for private beta.
meta.ogTitle: Vesta — Family life across countries, languages, and generations
meta.ogDescription: The AI-native family app for households that live across countries, languages, and generations. Built for expat families first.

hero.headline: Modern family life is more than one head can hold.
hero.subhead: The AI-powered family app that carries your family's mental load, so no one carries it alone.
hero.noSpam: No spam. No selling your data to anyone, ever.

s2.heading: It almost always falls on one person.
s2.intro: If you're the parent who knows when the school holiday starts, when your child's passport expires, and what's defrosting in the fridge — you're carrying the mental load of a modern family.

stats.text: of household mental-load tasks fall on one parent — regardless of income or career.
of mothers across Europe describe themselves as overloaded — nearly half report anxiety or depression linked to it.
of parents struggle to keep track of family events and commitments.
of families have missed something important because of a scheduling conflict.

s2.framing: It's most often a mother who carries it — but in plenty of homes it's simply whoever runs the logistics: the parent who travels less, the single earner, the one who quietly became the family's organiser. Whoever it is in your home, the weight is real — and it shouldn't sit on one set of shoulders.
s2.capstone: Vesta exists to take that load off your head — and put it somewhere your whole family can share it.

s3.heading: What a week actually looks like.
s3.subhead: Not a list of features — a few ordinary moments, and what Vesta quietly does with them.

cards.title: Your day, already sorted.
cards.vesta: Today in one view: swimming at 4, the parcel to sign for, dinner at your mum's — pulled from every household calendar, in your language.
cards.outcome: One glance instead of five apps and a mental tally.

cards.title: Two kids, two schedules.
cards.you: Snap the school flyers as they come home.
cards.vesta: Swimming, the dentist, the class-trip deposit due Friday — each on the right child's calendar, deadline and all.
cards.outcome: You stop being the family's data-entry clerk.

cards.title: A visit, finally on the calendar.
cards.you: Forward the message from your parents abroad about half-term.
cards.vesta: Reads the dates they suggest, lines them up against the kids' school holidays, and flags the week that works.
cards.outcome: A cross-border visit pinned down in seconds, not an email chain.

cards.title: See what's left to enjoy.
cards.you: Log the week's spending — "€45 groceries Lidl."
cards.vesta: "€980 of Fun Time left, 12 days to go — about €80 a day." And: "Mum's birthday next week — set money aside?"
cards.outcome: Budgeting that feels like permission, not restriction.

s4.heading: Four ideas that shape every screen.

principles.title: One person, many households.
principles.benefit: One login holds every household you belong to — your home, your parents' place abroad, a co-parent's home — so there's no separate app or group chat for each side of the family.
principles.brief: "Today, across your households: Noa's dentist at 4, your mum's flight lands at 2, and you're back from the Berlin trip Friday."

principles.title: Your family, in your language.
principles.benefit: Everyone sees the same family life in their own language and timezone — so no one's left out by a language or a clock that isn't theirs.
principles.brief: "A school notice arrives in Dutch. You read it in English, Grandma reads it in Turkish — and it lands in each calendar at the right local time."

principles.title: AI that does the thinking.
principles.benefit: Stop typing life into forms: snap a flyer, forward an email, or just say it — and the right calendars and to-do lists fill themselves in.
principles.brief: "Wednesday is stacked — Lily's swimming lesson, the weekly shop, and your usual catch-up with your parents. Want me to move the shop to Saturday morning so the evening feels less rushed?"

principles.title: A quiet, helpful partner.
principles.benefit: Help arrives when it actually counts and stays quiet when it doesn't — none of the badge-spam and notification noise other apps bury you in.
principles.brief: "A quiet day stays quiet. One note at six: 'Bins out tonight — otherwise, you're all caught up.'"

s5.heading: Built for families that don't fit in one country.
shapes: Parents abroad, grandparents back home, summers in a third country.
One parent travels for work most weeks; the other holds the home.
Co-parents across two countries with kids who move between both.
Cross-border families coordinating birthdays, school holidays, and visits across three timezones.
s5.graphBody: Message and coordinate with extended family who aren't in your household — your aunt, your child's other grandparent, your best friend who's basically family — without making them set up another account or join a new group chat.

s6.heading: GDPR by design. Your data, yours alone.
s6.subline: Vesta is independent, family-funded, and built for the long term.

items.desc: Your family's data lives in EU data centres.
Only the people you invite can see it.
Never used to train someone else's models.
Your activity stays inside your household, never followed across the web.

cta.heading: Be first into Vesta.
cta.body: Private beta opens to early waitlist members later in 2026. Tell us where you are and we'll prioritise beta invites accordingly.

items.a: A family management app — calendar, tasks, lists, photos, budget, and household chat — built around an AI that takes the mental load off the parent who's been carrying it. Mobile-first.
Modern families with multi-country, multilingual, or multi-household lives. If your family lives across countries, languages, or homes — Vesta is for you, expat or not.
We're currently in build, with private beta opening to early waitlist members in 2026. Public launch follows once we're confident the product holds up for real families.

items.q: How is it different from Apple Family Sharing or Google Family Link?
items.a: Those tools assume every family member shares the same home country. Vesta is built for families that live across countries, languages, and timezones from day one — and adds a real coordination layer on top: tasks, photos, household budget, and chat in one place.

items.q: How is it different from other family apps?
items.a: Vesta is European-built, multi-household by design (one account spans every household you belong to), multilingual per family member, AI-native, and GDPR-by-design. That combination is what makes Vesta the right fit for cross-border, multilingual, modern families.

items.q: Will it be available in my language?
items.a: Vesta launches in English, with Dutch following shortly after. German, French, and Turkish are next on the roadmap. Tell us your preferred language when you join the waitlist — we use that to prioritise.

items.q: Will my family's data be safe?
items.a: Yes. Vesta is GDPR-native, with EU data residency, no third-party tracking, and no selling of your data. Your household's data is scoped to your household, and you can export or delete it at any time.

items.q: What about elderly relatives who aren't tech-savvy?
items.a: Vesta is being designed so that a grandparent on a basic smartphone in another country can participate without an account-setup ordeal. Receiving photos, seeing a shared calendar, joining a family chat — that should be effortless on Day 1.

Vesta will have a free tier covering the essential coordination features for every family. Premium features will be priced fairly in EUR — we'll publish the details closer to launch. Waitlist members get extended free access to premium features during beta.

items.q: Are you hiring, fundraising, or open to partnerships?
items.a: We're independent and intend to stay that way. If you're an international school, an expat-community publisher, an HR mobility lead at a NL employer, or a relocation specialist — and you'd like to be involved in the beta — drop us a line at hello@vesta.social.

footer.tagline: Vesta — named after the Roman goddess of home and family.
